behavior_summary: U8P_LIGHT implements a reactive light-beam hit handler (U8P_LIGHT::gotHit) and a short suspend-driven timer/quake controller (U8P_LIGHT::func0080).

U8P_LIGHT::gotHit debounces repeated overlaps using the global alreadyHitLightBeam flag, inspects the light item's frame to choose behavior (either start a camera-quake timer or spawn a LightningBolt effect), plays an SFX, optionally applies damage via METHOD::receiveHitWrapper (e.g., when the source is the player), and may destroy the triggering item or spawn a cleanup process.

U8P_LIGHT::func0080 runs a suspended loop acting as a timer: it increments a tick counter each suspend, every 10 ticks computes a quake intensity (folding values >5), calls Camera::startQuake(intensity), and stops the effect with Camera::stopQuake when the counter reaches 100. On each tick there is a small random chance to call METHOD::receiveHitWrapper to inflict an incidental hit.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LIGHT::gotHit early-returns when alreadyHitLightBeam is set (guard/ debounce).

U8P_LIGHT::gotHit branches on Item::getFrame(this) to either: (a) initiate the quake path by spawning U8P_LIGHT::func0080 and a cleanup spawn, or (b) spawn a LightningBolt process for bolt-frame ranges.
U8P_LIGHT::func0080 loops via repeated suspend calls, using a local tick counter; every 10 ticks it updates quake intensity and at tick 100 it stops the quake.
Randomness in U8P_LIGHT::func0080 (urandom) creates a low-frequency chance to call receiveHitWrapper and apply damage during the quake timer.
open_questions: Exact numeric semantics and units of the quake 'intensity' parameter passed to Camera::startQuake (how intensity maps to visual effect).
Precise damage types/values and target selection semantics used when METHOD::receiveHitWrapper is invoked (inferred but not fully reconstructed).
The exact frame-to-behavior mapping boundaries (frames >=6 && <9 inferred for LightningBolt) — boundaries are inferred with medium confidence from remaining code fragments.

main_function.name: U8P_LIGHT::func0080
main_function.purpose: Run a short suspended timer that modulates camera quake intensity over ~100 ticks and occasionally applies a random incidental hit.
entry_conditions: Invoked by U8P_LIGHT::gotHit when a qualifying hit/overlap branch requests a quake animation/timer.
Once started it operates across repeated suspend ticks until the counter reaches 100 or is otherwise terminated.

function_map.name: U8P_LIGHT::func0080
function_map.role: suspend-driven timer/quake controller that periodically calls Camera::startQuake and Camera::stopQuake; occasionally invokes METHOD::receiveHitWrapper.

function_map.name: U8P_LIGHT::gotHit
function_map.role: overlap/hit handler that debounces via alreadyHitLightBeam, inspects Item::getFrame(this) to choose between starting the quake timer or spawning a LightningBolt, playing SFX, applying immediate damage, and cleaning up/destroying the triggering item.

internal_calls.purpose: Start the suspend-driven quake/timer when a hit frame/branch requires a camera-quake effect.

external_calls.purpose: Begin or update camera quake using a computed intensity (every 10 ticks).
Stop the camera quake when the timer completes (tick counter reaches 100).
Occasionally apply a random incidental hit/damage to a target during the quake timer (driven by urandom).
Drive the low-probability random-hit event on each tick.
Read the light item's current frame/index to decide which response path to take (quake vs LightningBolt).
Verify the supplied source/item actually overlaps the light beam before processing the hit.
Play a sound effect appropriate to the hit/branch for player feedback.
Apply immediate damage when the source parameter indicates the player (or when logic forces a direct hit).
Destroy/consume the triggering item when param semantics indicate it should be removed.
Spawn a short-lived cleanup process after handling the hit (inferred lifetime/cleanup helper).
Spawn a LightningBolt process/effect when the light frame falls into the bolt-range branch.

reads.name: alreadyHitLightBeam
reads.likely_effect: Guard/debounce check to avoid reprocessing an overlapping hit while handling is in progress.

writes.name: alreadyHitLightBeam
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 at the start of U8P_LIGHT::gotHit to prevent re-entrancy, and cleared to 0 at the end to allow future hits.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_LIGHT implements a reactive light-beam hit handler (U8P_LIGHT::gotHit) and a short suspend-driven timer/quake controller (U8P_LIGHT::func0080).
